_meta.description: Audit report structure template aligned with NACSA Direction No. 8 requirements for Act 854 CoP compliance audits

reportHeader.sector: [Government | Defence | Banking & Finance | Transportation | Healthcare | Water & Sewerage | Communication | Energy | Information & Communication Technology | Agriculture & Food]

tableOfContents.guidance: Auto-generated from section headings. Include page numbers for all sections and subsections.
sections: Appendix A: Prepared by Client (PBC) List
Appendix B: Sampling Methodology and Details
Appendix C: Glossary of Terms and Abbreviations

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary

ratingDefinitions.Compliant: The entity meets all material requirements of the applicable Code of Practice. No critical or high-severity findings identified. Any medium or low findings are minor in nature.
ratingDefinitions.SubstantiallyCompliant: The entity meets most requirements of the applicable Code of Practice. No critical findings identified. A limited number of high-severity findings exist, but compensating controls are in place to mitigate immediate risk.
ratingDefinitions.PartiallyCompliant: The entity has notable gaps in meeting the applicable Code of Practice. Critical or multiple high-severity findings have been identified that require prompt remediation.
ratingDefinitions.NonCompliant: The entity fails to meet fundamental requirements of the applicable Code of Practice. Multiple critical findings exist that pose an immediate risk to the security of NCII systems.

keyFindingsSummary.guidance: Summarize the most significant findings. Focus on critical and high-severity items. Limit to 5-7 key findings.

priorAuditComparison.guidance: If this is a follow-up or periodic audit, compare against prior audit results.
priorAuditComparison.newFindingsIdentified: [Count of new findings in current audit]

positiveObservations: [Notable areas of strong compliance or good practice observed]
areasRequiringAttention: [Summary of domains or areas needing immediate focus]

scopeAndMethodology.title: Scope and Methodology
scopeAndMethodology.auditObjective: [State the objective of the audit in accordance with Direction No. 8]

auditMethodology.approach: [Description of audit approach - risk-based, controls-based, or hybrid]
standardsFollowed: NACSA Direction No. 8 - Audit and Compliance Standards
auditMethodology.samplingApproach: [Description of sampling methodology - see Appendix B for details]

limitations: [Any limitations encountered during the audit that may affect conclusions]
scopeAndMethodology.entityCooperation: [Assessment of entity's cooperation and responsiveness during the audit]

entityOverview.entityType: [Government agency | GLC | Private sector | Critical infrastructure operator]

nciiContext.designationHistory: [When and how the entity was designated as NCII]
nciiContext.nciiSystemsSummary: [Summary description of NCII systems and their function]

governanceStructure.cisoOrEquivalent: [Name and reporting line of CISO or equivalent]

domainComplianceRatings.title: Domain Compliance Rating Summary
domainComplianceRatings.guidance: Rate each control domain assessed during the audit. Ratings should be consistent with findings documented in Section 5.

ratingScale.Effective: Controls are well-designed and operating effectively. No significant findings identified.
ratingScale.PartiallyEffective: Controls are in place but have notable gaps in design or operation. Medium-severity findings identified.
ratingScale.Ineffective: Controls are absent, poorly designed, or not operating. High or critical-severity findings identified.
ratingScale.NotAssessed: Domain was excluded from audit scope with documented justification.

detailedFindings.title: Detailed Findings
detailedFindings.guidance: Findings are organized by control domain. Each finding uses the 5 C's framework as defined in finding-template.json. Present findings in order of severity within each domain (Critical first, then High, Medium, Low).

findings.guidance: Insert completed finding templates here. Use the findingTemplate structure from finding-template.json.

vulnerabilityAndWeaknessSummary.title: Vulnerability and Weakness Summary
vulnerabilityAndWeaknessSummary.guidance: Consolidate technical vulnerabilities and systemic weaknesses identified across all domains. This section provides a holistic view of the entity's security posture.

technicalVulnerabilities.guidance: Summarize findings from vulnerability assessments, penetration tests, or configuration reviews referenced during the audit.

systemicWeaknesses.guidance: Identify patterns or recurring themes across multiple findings that indicate systemic issues.

weaknesses.recommendedApproach: [Strategic recommendation to address the systemic issue]

recommendationsSummary.title: Recommendations Summary
recommendationsSummary.guidance: Consolidate all recommendations from findings into a prioritized action plan. Group by priority and provide a clear roadmap for remediation.

priorityGroups.description: Actions required to address critical findings that pose immediate risk to NCII systems.
Actions required to address high-severity findings and strengthen control effectiveness.
Actions to address medium-severity findings and improve overall compliance posture.
Low-priority observations and improvement opportunities to be addressed in the normal course of operations.

priorAuditComparison.title: Prior Audit Comparison
priorAuditComparison.guidance: For follow-up or periodic audits, provide a detailed comparison with the prior audit. For initial audits, note 'Not applicable - initial audit' and skip this section.

priorFindingsDisposition.currentStatus: [Remediated | Partially Remediated | Not Remediated | No Longer Applicable]
priorFindingsDisposition.currentFindingRef: [Current finding ID if still open, or N/A]

newFindingsSummary.narrative: [Brief description of new findings not present in prior audit]

overallComplianceOpinion.title: Overall Compliance Opinion
overallComplianceOpinion.guidance: Provide the auditor's formal compliance opinion in accordance with Direction No. 8 requirements.
overallComplianceOpinion.opinionStatement: [Formal auditor opinion statement on the entity's compliance with the applicable Code of Practice]
overallComplianceOpinion.basisForOpinion: [Summary of the basis for the opinion, referencing key findings and overall assessment]
overallComplianceOpinion.emphasis: [Any matters the auditor wishes to emphasize without qualifying the opinion]
overallComplianceOpinion.qualifications: [Any qualifications or limitations to the opinion, if applicable]
overallComplianceOpinion.nacsaRecommendation: [Auditor's recommendation to NACSA regarding the entity's compliance status and any recommended regulatory actions]

appendixA.title: Prepared by Client (PBC) List
appendixA.guidance: Document all evidence and documentation requested from the entity, including receipt status.

pbcItems.status: [Received | Partially Received | Not Received | Not Applicable]
pbcItems.notes: [Any relevant notes about the evidence received]

appendixB.title: Sampling Methodology and Details
appendixB.guidance: Document the sampling approach and specific samples selected for each test procedure.

terms.definition: National Cyber Security Act 2024 (Act 854) - The primary legislation governing cyber security of national critical information infrastructure in Malaysia
Chief Executive of the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA)
Code of Practice - Standards issued by NACSA specifying cyber security measures for NCII entities
NACSA direction establishing audit and compliance standards for NCII entities
Management Action Plan - Entity's plan to remediate audit findings
National Cyber Security Agency - The regulatory body responsible for cyber security of NCII in Malaysia
National Critical Information Infrastructure - Computer or computer system designated under s23 of Act 854
Prepared by Client - Evidence and documentation requested from the audited entity

appendixD.title: Auditor Qualifications
appendixD.guidance: Document the qualifications and experience of the audit team in accordance with Direction No. 8 requirements.

teamQualifications.name: [Full name]
teamQualifications.role: [Lead Auditor | Audit Team Member | Technical Specialist]

appendixE.title: Independence Declaration
appendixE.guidance: Formal declaration of auditor independence as required by Direction No. 8.

declaration.statement: We hereby declare that the audit team members listed in this report are independent of the audited entity and have no conflicts of interest that would impair our objectivity or professional judgment in conducting this audit.
confirmations: No financial interest in the audited entity
No prior employment with the audited entity within the past 24 months
No provision of non-audit services to the audited entity that could compromise independence
No family or personal relationships with key personnel of the audited entity
No other circumstances that could reasonably be perceived as impairing independence

appendixF.title: Entity Management Sign-Off
appendixF.guidance: Management acknowledgment of audit findings and commitment to the Management Action Plan.

signOff.statement: We acknowledge receipt of this audit report and confirm that the findings have been discussed with the audit team. We accept the findings and have committed to the Management Action Plan as detailed in the accompanying MAP tracker document.
